topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up, a jarring intrusion on an otherwise quiet afternoon. A profile picture loaded: a grinning, familiar face that instantly tightened something cold in my chest. It was Mark. Mark, who used to orchestrate my humiliation in the third grade with the precision of a seasoned artist. My jaw clenched so hard I could taste metal. I stared at the request, my thumb hovering over the 'decline' button, a slow, deliberate movement fueled by years of resentment. Why now? What did he want? A misplaced sense of curiosity, perhaps, or just the need to see if the old dynamic still held any power? I closed the app without responding, the silence in my room amplifying the dull throb behind my eyes.

[story 2]

I saw the request flicker across my screen, and a familiar knot formed in my stomach, tight and unpleasant. It was him. The ghost from my formative years, reappearing in my digital space. A wave of heat rose up my neck, and I instinctively pulled my hoodie tighter around myself, hunching my shoulders as if bracing for a blow. *He thinks this is funny,* I thought, a sharp, acidic retort forming unspoken in my mind. I didn't even bother to check his name; I just watched the little dot indicating his pending connection, a silent acknowledgment of the past colliding violently with the present. I deleted the notification immediately, wanting the digital space to be as empty and unblemished as possible.

[story 3]

When the alert chimed, I didn't look up immediately. I was meticulously sorting through old photographs, trying to bury the memory of that boy under layers of dust and faded paper. Then I saw it—Mark’s profile. A slow, almost imperceptible tremor ran through my hand, causing the photograph to slip from my grasp. My breath hitched, shallow and quick. I felt a profound weariness settle over me, a heavy cloak of exhaustion born from enduring too much. A muscle twitched in my cheek. I didn't want contact; I wanted distance, a clean severing of the thread connecting me to that unpleasant era. I navigated to the settings, my fingers moving with a detached, almost mechanical precision, and blocked the incoming invitation without a second thought.
